title: The Slow Undoing
intention: To let the day come loose and drift gently down into sleep.

lines.text: Welcome. There is nothing left to do now but be here.
Let the bed hold you. It has been waiting all day.
Feel where you are heaviest, and let that part go first.
There is no next thing. Only this breath, already slowing.
Breathe in, soft and unhurried.
And let it leave, like a tide that asks nothing of you.
Again. In, quietly.
Out, even slower than before.
Let your shoulders forget the shape they held all day.
Let your jaw come loose. Let the space behind your eyes go dim.
Your hands are open now. They are holding nothing.
The day is a coat. Let it slip from your shoulders to the floor.
Whatever you carried, set it at the edge of the bed.
It will keep. It does not need you tonight.
Imagine a small warm light, low on the horizon.
It is dimming, the way a window dims at dusk.
Soften your breath toward it.
Each breath out, the light sinks a little lower.
Your legs grow heavy and warm, sinking into the dark.
Your back is held. Your chest rises and falls without your help.
Thoughts may still drift by. Let them pass like slow clouds.
You do not have to follow them. You can simply watch them go.
The room is quieter now. The edges of things go soft.
Even the light is almost gone. Just a warm ember, far away.
Let your breath grow smaller. Let it almost disappear.
There is nowhere to be. There is no morning yet.
Only the weight of you, settling deeper into the dark.
Let the words come undone. They no longer need to mean.
Let my voice grow distant, like a tide going out.
You are safe. You are warm. You are already drifting.
Down, and down, slow as falling snow.
Nothing to hold. Nothing to keep.
Let go.
Sleep now.
